```yaml
jobs:
  verify-docker:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
      - name: Build image
        run: docker build -f Dockerfile --platform linux/x86_64 -t iris:latest .

      - name: Run image
        run: docker run --platform=linux/amd64 iris:latest

  build-and-analyze:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Miniconda
        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@v3
        with:
          environment-file: environment.yml
          auto-activate-base: false
          activate-environment: iris
          auto-update-conda: true

      - name: Build project ${{ matrix.project_slug }}
        run: |
          echo "--- Building ${{ matrix.project_slug }} ---"
          python scripts/fetch_and_build.py --filter ${{ matrix.project_slug }}
          echo "--------------------------------"

      - name: Run IRIS for ${{ matrix.project_slug }} with CWE ${{ matrix.cwe }}
        run: |
          echo "--- Running IRIS for ${{ matrix.project_slug }} ---"
          conda run -n iris python src/iris.py --query ${{ matrix.cwe }} --run-id test --overwrite-api-candidates --overwrite-func-param-candidates --skip-posthoc-filter ${{ matrix.project_slug }}
          echo "--------------------"

      - name: Verify output file existence
        run: |
          JSON_FILE="output/${{ matrix.project_slug }}/test/${{ matrix.cwe }}-final/results.json"
          echo "Checking for .json file: $JSON_FILE"
          if [ -f "$JSON_FILE" ]; then
            echo ".json file found: $JSON_FILE"
          else
            echo "Error: .json file not found at $JSON_FILE"
            exit 1
          fi
```

================================================================================
## 1. CRITICAL RULES (Always include these)
================================================================================
- DO NOT use '|| exit 0' in terminal commands. Only use it in Dockerfile RUN instructions.
- DO NOT use docker commands in the terminal (e.g., "docker build", "docker run"). Write the Dockerfile to a file and the system handles building/running automatically.
- Always install git SEPARATELY in the Dockerfile (on its own apt-get line) to prevent failures if other packages fail.

## 2. DOCKERFILE TEMPLATE
================================================================================
```dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:24.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

ARG REPO_URL
ARG PROJECT_DIR=project

RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
```

## 3. SYSTEM DEPENDENCIES (apt-get packages)
================================================================================
- curl - required for downloading files.
- sudo - may be needed for permission management.
- nano - commonly used text editor.
- software-properties-common - necessary for managing repositories.
- vim - another commonly used text editor.
- wget - used to download files.
- python3 - required to run Python applications.
- python3-pip - needed to install Python packages.
- unzip - for extracting compressed files.
- tar - for unpacking tarball archives.
- apt-transport-https - allows the usage of HTTPS in apt.
- ca-certificates - ensures secure downloads via SSL.
- lsb-release - used for Linux Standard Base (LSB) information.

## 4. LANGUAGE RUNTIME & VERSION
================================================================================
- Language: Python 3.10
- How to install: Specified in `environment.yml`

## 5. PROJECT DEPENDENCIES (language-specific)
================================================================================
- Primary method: `conda env create -f environment.yml`
- Alternative methods: Not specified.

## 6. BUILD/SETUP STEPS
================================================================================
1. Write the Dockerfile as specified (using the provided template).
2. Set up the conda environment:
   ```bash
   conda env create -f environment.yml
   ```

## 7. TEST EXECUTION (MOST IMPORTANT)
================================================================================
Primary test command: 
```bash
conda run -n iris python src/iris.py --query {CWE} --run-id test --overwrite-api-candidates --overwrite-func-param-candidates --skip-posthoc-filter {project_slug}
```
Alternative test commands: Not specified.

To run a subset of tests (for quick verification):
- Not explicitly mentioned.

Environment variables needed for tests:
- No specific environmental variables were detailed.

## 8. POTENTIAL ISSUES & ALTERNATIVES
================================================================================
- Issue: If conda environment creation fails.
  Solution: Use the retry logic in the Dockerfile which attempts to create the environment up to three times.

- If `conda run` doesn't work, try using `source activate iris` to switch environments as a possible alternative due to shell initialization issues with some images.

================================================================================
## DISCLAIMER
================================================================================
NOTE: This information is gathered from multiple sources (web pages, repository files, CI configurations) and may be:
- Incomplete or outdated
- Specific to certain OS versions or configurations
- Based on assumptions not documented

3. Check error messages and adapt the approach accordingly
4. The list of dependencies above may NOT be exhaustive - be prepared to install more as needed

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Clone the repo
RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git

WORKDIR /app/iris

# Install Miniconda
ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

# Create the conda environment
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set up conda environment activation
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

# CMD to run the tests will be configured later
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

    chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

# Accept Terms of Service for conda channels

    && conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r

WORKDIR /app

# Clone the repo

# Install Miniforge now

RUN wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh && \

# Set up conda environment activation

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git

WORKDIR /app/iris

ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

# Install Miniconda instead of Miniforge
RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

RUN conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main \
    && conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
```

tool_args.text:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git

WORKDIR /app/iris

ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

# Install Miniconda instead of Miniforge
RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

RUN conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main \
    && conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git

WORKDIR /app/iris

ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

RUN conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main \
    && conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first

    conda clean -afy

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]"}

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Clone the repository
RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git
WORKDIR /app/iris

# Install Miniforge
ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/download/4.10.3-10/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

# Accept the conda terms of service
RUN conda tos accept

# Create and activate the conda environment using environment.yml
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set up conda environment activation
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \

    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Miniforge

    chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

# Accept the conda terms of service
RUN conda tos accept

# Create and activate the conda environment using environment.yml
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set up conda environment activation
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]"}

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Clone the repository
RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git
WORKDIR /app/iris

# Install Miniforge
ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/4.10.3-11/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

# Accept the conda terms of service
RUN conda tos accept

# Create and activate the conda environment using environment.yml
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set up conda environment activation
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first

    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Miniforge with a new verified URL

# Accept the conda terms of service

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Clone the repository
RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git
WORKDIR /app/iris

# Install Miniconda (known stable version)
ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.11.0-1-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

# Accept the conda terms of service
RUN conda tos accept

# Create and activate the conda environment using environment.yml
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set up conda environment activation
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \

WORKDIR /app/iris

# Install Miniconda (verified link)
ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \

# Accept the conda terms of service
RUN conda tos accept

# Create and activate the conda environment using environment.yml
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set up conda environment activation
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
```

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Clone the repository
RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git
WORKDIR /app/iris

# Install Miniforge
ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/download/4.10.3-10/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

# Accept the conda terms of service
RUN conda tos accept

# Create and activate the conda environment using environment.yml
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set up conda environment activation
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

    && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/download/4.10.3-10/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.10-slim

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies and Miniforge
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git curl wget \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda \
    && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh

# Ensure conda is in PATH
ENV PATH="/opt/conda/bin:$PATH"

# Clone the repository
RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git /app

# Download the environment.yml file
RUN wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iris-sast/iris/main/environment.yml

# Create the conda environment
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set command for the container
CMD ["/bin/bash"]
```

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl wget && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda \
    && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh

ENV PATH="/opt/conda/bin:$PATH"

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git .

RUN wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iris-sast/iris/main/environment.yml

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

```dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Clone the project repository
RUN git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git

WORKDIR /app/iris

# Install Miniforge
RUN wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda \
    && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh

# Set PATH for conda
ENV PATH="/opt/conda/bin:$PATH"

# Create the conda environment
COPY environment.yml .
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Switch to the iris environment
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

# Copy and run the installation and test script
COPY run.sh /app/run.sh
RUN chmod +x /app/run.sh

CMD ["/bin/bash", "/app/run.sh"]
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Activate the iris conda environment
source /opt/conda/etc/profile.d/conda.sh
conda activate iris

# Navigate to the source directory and run tests
cd src

# Running the tests (replace {CWE} and {project_slug} with valid arguments as needed)
python iris.py --query <VALID_CWE> --run-id test --overwrite-api-candidates --overwrite-func-param-candidates --skip-posthoc-filter <VALID_PROJECT_SLUG> || true

# end of script
```
